lines.text: Welcome back. [pause] It's {{CLOCK}}. We're {{ELAPSED}} in.
I want you to notice the silence first. [pause] Not the absence of sound. The silence that's underneath.
Breathe in through your nose. [pause] The air is cold tonight. Hold that. [pause] Release.
Your shoulders are high. They've been high for hours. Drop them now.
This is the hour when most people stop moving. [pause] The ones outside. The ones inside.
Scan down from your neck. Notice your chest. Is it tight? [pause] That's correct.
The fog rolled in off Lake Mendota around 01:00. Caroline said that. [pause] The visibility dropped to one and a half miles. Do you feel it?
Breathe in. [pause] The Sisters say that fog is the world forgetting its own edges.
Your arms are heavy. Let them be. [pause] They don't need to do anything right now.
Someone on Atwood Avenue is sitting in their kitchen with the lights off. [pause] Not because the power failed. Because the dark is safer.
Scan to your stomach. What do you feel? [pause] Hunger. Nausea. Absence.
The kill count at this hour last year was one hundred and thirty-nine. [pause] Tucker didn't say that number tonight. Did you notice?
Breathe in. [pause] Four count. [pause] Release.
There are twenty-three people still missing from 2168. [pause] Twenty-three people who did not come home and no one found them.
Your legs are part of the earth. Feel them. [pause] Heavy. Rooted.
I'm going to ask you something and you don't need to answer out loud.
Who are you protecting by staying in this room? [pause] Yourself or them?
Kalimoknakeshti. [pause] Release that word. It's not for you.
Breathe. The ones outside are not your responsibility. [pause] The ones outside made their choice.
Last year a woman called from near Tenney Park. [pause] She didn't give her name. She just wanted to hear someone speak.
We played a song for her. The Bouncing Souls. [pause] She stayed on the line.
I don't know if she's listening right now. [pause] If you are — the light is coming.
Four hours and eighteen minutes from now, the fog will start to lift. [pause] Caroline was wrong about the timing, but the fog will lift.
Breathe in the cold. [pause] It's cleaning you.
Your jaw is clenched. I can hear it through the speakers. [pause] Unclench. Your teeth are safe.
The Capitol is still locked down. [pause] The Director is still inside. [pause] He's watching the same clock you're watching.
But he's inside stone and glass. [pause] You're inside the thing that matters.
Body scan. Feet to crown. Where does it hurt? [pause] There. That spot.
You've been holding something since 22:00. Maybe since before the Purge started. [pause] Put it down. Just for the next six minutes.
Breathe in. [pause] The Sisters taught me a word for the thing you're feeling right now.
It's not fear. [pause] It's clarity.
Vokkalathemkesh. [pause] The Sisters say it means 'the lightness of having nothing left to lose.'
That's not true. [pause] But it means something.
Your heartbeat is the only law in this room. [pause] Listen to it.
The silence outside — that's the worst part, isn't it? [pause] Not the screaming. The quiet after.
Breathe in. [pause] That silence is not your fault.
Four hours of Purge left. [pause] The deepest part of the night. The part where the mind goes strange.
If you're crying right now, that's correct. [pause] Tears are a form of breathing.
I'm going to ask you to do something difficult. [pause] Forgive yourself for being afraid.
Not for being here. [pause] For being human while here.
Breathe in. [pause] The fog remembers every footstep. [pause] The cold remembers every choice.
But you — you're allowed to forget. [pause] Tomorrow morning you're allowed to forget this hour ever happened.
The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. [pause] That's just a number.
What's real is the breath. [pause] In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
Stay in that rhythm. [pause] Stay there until the light comes.
The Sisters are listening. [pause] I'm listening. [pause] The ones on the Beltline and the East Side and the lake — they're listening too, in their way.
You are not alone in the dark. [pause] You are alone with the dark. [pause] There's a difference.
When you leave this meditation, the night will still be there. [pause] But you'll be different.
Breathe in. [pause] The light is four hours away.
That's not a promise. [pause] That's a fact.

tickerItems.text: State Street Ruins: unusually quiet. No reports since 00:30. Pattern unknown.
Lake Mendota water temp: 24°F. Hypothermia threshold: 15 min. Do not attempt crossing.
Clarity is not the opposite of fear.
Fog advisory: visibility 1-2 miles Mendota shoreline through 05:00. Lifting 05:30 EST.
